title: elymus novae-angliae
intro: Elymus novae-angliae, also known as New England wild rye, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. This plant is used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Elymus novae-angliae include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting digestive health. Elymus novae-angliae can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or salve, and is often used in traditional herbal remedies to promote overall well-being.
